title: Does the soul equal the mind
display_title: Does the soul equal the mind
wikitext:
[]: Does the soul equal the mind? Post by Zephros » Mon Mar 08, 2010 12:58 am When I first tried to understand Evangelion years ago I ran into a disparity when it talked about souls I could understand a soul being placed in an Eva, but not a partial soul. I was raised christian so I guess I have that mentality where a soul is not divisible. Plus, they were liking individuality to a soul, where for me that lies in the mind. I guess that is where my original confusion stemmed, and for years I could not reconcile that logic in the series. Yet, now I think I can. I've looked, but have had no luck is this next part. Is there perchance some way in the translation that in the Japanese version the word they used is interchangeable for mind? I doubt it, but it's worth a shot in asking. Now to the meat. Somewhere in the series someone said the body is the throne of the soul. Okay. It is where the soul sits. I literally imagine a soul sitting on the body, which lead to this revelation, for me at least. That the soul they seem to talk about so freely in the series is not the soul, as most christians see it, but really the mind they are referencing. So whenever they are saying soul, they are meaning mind. The mind can be broken apart and separated because it has parts, which is why the motherly part of Asuka's mom could go into Unit 02. Or am I looking at it wrong? That maybe Anno is of the belief that the soul and mind are different, just that what makes us emotional and well...us, is also reflected in the soul? I tried looking for a discussion on this, but to no avail. So I'm putting my thoughts out there to see what fans are thought of it.

[]: Post by Azathoth » Mon Mar 08, 2010 1:11 am Zephros wrote: I've looked, but have had no luck is this next part. Is there perchance some way in the translation that in the Japanese version the word they used is interchangeable for mind? I doubt it, but it's worth a shot in asking.

[]: Post by Allemann » Mon Mar 08, 2010 1:15 am Going by the show, the souls are human minds, various inconsistencies notwithstanding.

[]: Post by Octahedron » Mon Mar 08, 2010 3:14 am I always wondered how they can copy "thought patterns" (the MAGI system and the Dummy Plugs), but not souls. So the two must be a separate concept. Yet transferring a soul will also result in transferance of memories. Since Evas have human souls, but not human thought patterns, I wonder how human they actually are (some people seem to portray them as essentially being the same people the souls belonged to, only giant robots).

[]: Post by Xard » Mon Mar 08, 2010 4:49 am thought patterns =/= consciousness I guess you could record anyone's brain activity and then build model based on those "thought patterns"

[]: Post by Halicat » Mon Mar 08, 2010 5:20 am Octahedron wrote: I wonder how human they actually are (some people seem to portray them as essentially being the same people the souls belonged to, only giant robots).

[]: Post by Reichu » Mon Mar 08, 2010 6:42 am The show (and external materials, of course) refers to whatever is inside the Evas as being both "tamashii" (soul) and "kokoro" (mind/heart). Even Kaworu says that the A.T. Field is "kokoro no kabe" (wall of the mind/heart). The concepts of soul and mind/heart are either being treated as same, or one includes the other. For purposes of NGE, I've been inclined to conceptualize the soul as the whole of the "metaphysical organism", and the mind as the part of the soul embodying self and consciousness. A body-brain kind of analog, I suppose. Whether this has any resemblance to original intentions, I have no idea. Somewhere in the series someone said the body is the throne of the soul.

[]: Post by Uriel Septim VII » Mon Mar 08, 2010 6:30 pm I like to equate NGE souls to philotes, where they are on their own nothing special, but are required to spark life, making them the key to separating living entities from non-living. As for those Rei clones not having souls, that's a case of a philosophical zombie: they're like robots, except not only are beyond biomechanical, they are made from the exact same matter as life forms.

[]: Post by Grimmjow » Mon Mar 08, 2010 6:41 pm When the body dies, the mind ceases to be. However, we see in instrumentality the souls of the dead being gathered into Lilith's egg. Hence, imo, the mind and soul are separate in terms of Eva mythos.

[]: Post by Xard » Mon Mar 08, 2010 6:53 pm Grimmjow wrote: When the body dies, the mind ceases to be. However, we see in instrumentality the souls of the dead being gathered into Lilith's egg. Hence, imo, the mind and soul are separate in terms of Eva mythos.

[]: Post by Eva Yojimbo » Mon Mar 08, 2010 8:45 pm Reichu wrote: Even Kaworu says that the A.T. Field is "kokoro no kabe" (wall of the mind/heart).

[]: Post by Reichu » Mon Mar 08, 2010 9:16 pm Grimmjow wrote: When the body dies, the mind ceases to be. However, we see in instrumentality the souls of the dead being gathered into Lilith's egg. Hence, imo, the mind and soul are separate in terms of Eva mythos.

[]: Post by Octahedron » Tue Mar 09, 2010 1:47 am If you consider soul to be something that gives you sentience, then bacterias and trees having souls would be odd. However, maybe in NGE a soul is the "life force" or organisms, something that makes them alive and allows them to have an AT field that maintains their physical form. In that case even simple lifeforms having something that could be called a soul would make sense.

[]: Post by Reichu » Tue Mar 09, 2010 3:24 am Octahedron wrote: If you consider soul to be something that gives you sentience, then bacterias and trees having souls would be odd. However, maybe in NGE a soul is the "life force" or organisms, something that makes them alive and allows them to have an AT field that maintains their physical form.

[]: Post by chaosakita » Tue Mar 09, 2010 4:55 pm Reichu wrote: Lots of gaijin fans seem to have a problem with the notion of microbes and plants with souls. Something to do with the general divide between western and eastern spirituality?

[]: Post by Ornette » Tue Mar 09, 2010 5:10 pm I just asked a Japanese native: according to Japanese Buddhism, yes, every living thing has a soul. that is one of the reasons for saying 'itadakimasu' before eating. you are honouring the life of the thing you are eating.

[]: Post by Zephros » Tue Mar 09, 2010 5:58 pm Well, this certainly bloomed into a nice debate. I think before this goes into the debate of whether or not microrganisms have souls I think it's best to define soul/mind/heart/body. This analysis is going to be based on the ideas expressed here plus the quotes from the anime that have been provided. Body, that which holds the mind, body, and heart. I think that it something we can all agree on. The heart came up from Kaworu's quote, wall of heart/mind. Does kokoro actually encompass both of those? As it means heart/mind? There seemed to be some doubt? Someone also said due to episode 21 we know all life forms have a soul. I could not find the quote. Was it from one of the deleted scenes? Does anyone have it to confirm this? (My theory is going to be based on the assumption of it's validity.) In the EoE an anti-AT Field took down everyone's field. This is fact. I'm going to stretch this next part, but to me I've always assumed to have an AT field meant to have a soul. So if it is the wall, in humans and such, that keeps everyone seperate, then it surrounds the soul. Thus, mind in a sense does equal soul. Going by that logic. This seems to contradict what they said in the retrieving Shinji. IBUKI: We can surmise that Shinji's body lost its ego border, and he's floating in the entry plug in quantum form.

[]: Post by planet news » Tue Mar 09, 2010 8:51 pm Zephros wrote: Now onto the last part. The Soul. The Soul I think is life force, the will to live, spirit. But also more than that. The Soul, I think in the series, is also the Super-Ego. Thus, I also think emotion is derived from the soul. I did say it was part of the mind, but from my logic here the mind is actually being split between the soul and the body. Where the Id lies, without any references from the movie, I would say in the body.

[]: Post by Zephros » Tue Mar 09, 2010 10:51 pm planet news wrote: Is this what you think, or what you think Evangelion/Anno "thinks" the soul is? As a person "of science," I reject all notion of there being anything even remotely like a soul, i.e. organ called brain = flashing lights = illusion of consciousness that works. However, I think from everything you've gathered, Evangelion sees the soul as that "essential element" in all people that is "who they are", disregarding the mind and the body entirely. The soul is what a person "truly is, deep down." So the mother souls in Eva are what the mother "truly" is. The soul is like a kind of energy force that is unique to each person and "lets them" be. To equate the soul to a personality (the mind/psyche) is pretty much ridiculous in Evangelion terms. When the AT fields were downed, their bodies disintegrated. They didn't just lift up "out of" their bodies like ghosts do in so many movies before. I think this was an agreement to what you said??

[]: Post by Octahedron » Wed Mar 10, 2010 11:40 am I think Evangelion's view on souls might be something of a mix between eastern and western views. As mentioned above, in eastern religions it's believed that every living being has a soul. In Catholism at least (not quite sure about other secs of Christianity) soul is what gives us sapience, so a human has a soul but animals don't. In Evangelion every living being has a soul, but "soul" is also used interchangably along with "mind" and "psyche". My quess is that not all souls in Evaverse are equal. Humans and ants both have brains, but human brain is much more complex. Perhaps human soul is also much more complex than an ant soul, so while a human's soul gives them sapience and a distinct psyche and personality (in addition to maintaining their individual form via the AT-Field), an ant's soul just allows the ant to maintain it's individual form, but does not give it an individual mind or personality.

In Catholism at least (not quite sure about other secs of Christianity) soul is what gives us sapience, so a human has a soul but animals don't.
